senses.definition: the process of converting or being converted into carbon, especially charcoal or graphite, by heating with limited oxygen
examples: The carbonisation of the wood produced a high-charcoal yield.
Industrial carbonisation occurs at controlled temperatures to produce inert carbon forms.

senses.definition: plural of carbonisation; instances or processes of converting into carbon in multiple cases
examples: Several carbonisations were observed at different stages of the reactor.
The lab compared several carbonisations to optimise yield.

senses.definition: to convert or burn (a substance) to carbon, often through heating with limited oxygen
examples: Wood is carbonised to produce charcoal.
The process carbonises the material without fully combusting it.

senses.definition: made into carbon or charcoal by carbonisation; scorched or burned to blackened carbon
examples: The carbonised wood emitted a distinct smell.
A carbonised layer formed on the surface after heating.

senses.definition: a device or person that carbonises; a furnace or apparatus used for carbonisation
examples: The carboniser in the plant operates at precise temperatures.
Researchers tested a new carboniser design for improved efficiency.

senses.definition: plural of carboniser; devices or people that carbonise
examples: Several carbonisers were installed to handle the supply chain.
Different carbonisers yield varying purities of carbon product.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of carbonise; causes or undergoes carbonisation
examples: The process carbonises the material slowly.
This method carbonises the substrate, forming a protective layer.

senses.definition: present participle of carbonise; the process of converting to carbon
examples: The material is carbonising under limited oxygen conditions.
Carbonising occurs before the final heat treatment.

senses.definition: a type of carbon compound, often used to refer to a clay or mineral containing carbon; in some contexts a fictional or historical material
examples: The ore contained traces of carbonite.
Carbonite crystals formed after the reaction.

senses.definition: a compound consisting of carbon and nitrogen, often a ceramic or refractory material
examples: Researchers studied carbonitride ceramics for high-temperature applications.
Carbonitride coatings improve wear resistance.

senses.definition: an old term historically used for a positively charged carbon ion; now largely obsolete or literary
examples: In old texts, carbonium was described as a reactive ion.
The theory of carbonium ions appeared in early organic chemistry literature.

senses.definition: plural of carbonium; historical or literary references to carbonium ions
examples: The manuscript mentions several carboniums in reaction schemes.
Carboniums are discussed in older inorganic chemistry texts.

senses.definition: capable of being carbonised or turned into carbon
examples: The material is highly carbonizable under heat treatment.
Carbonizable substances yield good charcoal.

senses.definition: the process of carbonisation; the conversion of substance into carbon
examples: Carbonization occurs during pyrolysis.
The furnace increases efficiency during carbonization.

senses.definition: plural of carbonization; instances or processes of carbonizing
examples: The study compared several carbonizations of the sample.
Different carbonizations yield varying carbon contents.

senses.definition: to convert into carbon or charcoal by heating in the absence of air
examples: They used heat to carbonize the piece of wood.
The process can carbonize organic material efficiently.

senses.definition: made into carbon or charcoal by carbonization; blackened by burning
examples: The carbonized wood crumbled easily.
A carbonized crust protected the inner material.

senses.definition: a device or person that carbonizes; a furnace or tool used to carbonize
examples: New carbonizers were installed to increase efficiency.
The operator adjusted the carbonizer to maintain consistent temperatures.

senses.definition: plural of carbonizer; devices or people that carbonize
examples: The plant added several carbonizers to handle the load.
Different carbonizers produce different product qualities.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of carbonize; causes to become carbon
examples: The material carbonizes under heat.
This process carbonizes the surface, creating a protective layer.

senses.definition: present participle of carbonize; process of turning something into carbon
examples: Carbonizing occurs before final processing.
The material is carbonizing under controlled conditions.

senses.definition: not containing carbon; lacking carbon; lacking drawings or traces of carbon paper
examples: The report is carbonless and easy to photocopy.
They used carbonless copy paper for the form.

senses.definition: a Belgian beef and onion stew, traditionally prepared with beer and rich gravy; a regional dish
examples: We enjoyed traditional carbonnade flamande for dinner.
Carbonnade is often served with fries or bread to soak up the sauce.

senses.definition: plural of carbonnade; multiple servings or varieties of carbonnade
examples: The menu featured several carbonnades from different regions.
Carbonnades vary in spice and beer used.

senses.definition: a proper noun likely referring to a surname or place (usage limited/rare)
examples: We studied works by Carbonnieux in the French collection.
Carbonnieux is not a common English term outside specific contexts.
collocations: Carbonnieux winery (if referring to a place)
